section: Chapter 9: The Amazing World of Solutes, Solvents, and Solutions

questions.question: What is a solution?
questions.correct_answer: A uniform mixture formed when a solute dissolves in a solvent
questions.explanation: In a solution, the components are evenly distributed and cannot be distinguished separately.

questions.question: What is a solute?
questions.correct_answer: The substance that dissolves in a solvent to form a solution
questions.explanation: Solutes are usually present in smaller amounts in a solution.

questions.question: What is a solvent?
questions.correct_answer: The substance that dissolves the solute in a solution
questions.explanation: The solvent is usually present in larger quantity in a solution.

questions.question: What is a saturated solution?
questions.correct_answer: A solution in which no more solute can dissolve at a given temperature
questions.explanation: Any additional solute remains undissolved once saturation is reached.

questions.question: What is an unsaturated solution?
questions.correct_answer: A solution that can still dissolve more solute at a given temperature
questions.explanation: Unsaturated solutions have not yet reached their dissolving limit.

questions.question: What is solubility?
questions.correct_answer: The maximum amount of solute that can dissolve in a fixed quantity of solvent at a particular temperature
questions.explanation: Solubility determines how much solute can form a solution with a solvent.

questions.question: How does temperature affect solubility of solids?
questions.correct_answer: The solubility of most solids increases with increasing temperature
questions.explanation: Heating allows more particles of the solute to dissolve in the solvent.

questions.question: What is density?
questions.correct_answer: The mass of a substance per unit volume
questions.explanation: Density helps determine whether substances float or sink in liquids.

questions.question: What is the formula for density?
questions.explanation: Density is calculated by dividing the mass of a substance by its volume.

questions.question: Why do some objects float while others sink in water?
questions.correct_answer: Because of differences in density between the object and water
questions.explanation: Objects less dense than water float, while denser objects sink.

notes.title: Solutions
notes.content: A solution is a uniform mixture where the solute dissolves evenly in the solvent.

notes.title: Saturated and Unsaturated Solutions
notes.content: A saturated solution cannot dissolve more solute, while an unsaturated solution can dissolve additional solute.

notes.title: Solubility and Temperature
notes.content: The solubility of most solids increases with temperature, but gases become less soluble in liquids at higher temperatures.

notes.title: Density
notes.content: Density is the mass of a substance divided by its volume and helps explain floating and sinking behavior.

notes.title: Measuring Mass and Volume
notes.content: Mass is measured using a balance and volume is measured using a measuring cylinder.

facts.fact: Air is considered a solution because different gases are uniformly mixed together.
Cold water can dissolve more oxygen than warm water, which is important for aquatic life.
Density is usually measured in units such as g/cm³ or kg/m³.
Hot air rises because heated air becomes less dense than the surrounding cooler air.
